title: Is it possible to use the Email column as the email address for sending out notifications?
excerpt: We are using a form to capture the email address during data entry. We would like to use that email address as the emailto: portion of an automation. We are not seeing that as an option in the notification setup.

posts.author: jdavies-IDD (John Davies)
posts.cookedHtml: We are using a form to capture the email address during data entry. We would like to use that email address as the emailto: portion of an automation. We are not seeing that as an option in the notification setup.

posts.author: agranville (Andre Granville)
posts.cookedHtml:
Onde vote for this one. I’m in need of this feature also.
Actually my email column is a mirror column. I need to use this mirror column in my automation for sending emails.

posts.author: CFcut (Celia Fraser)
posts.cookedHtml:
We’ve been asking for this as well for a while. But it’s still not possible. At the moment we have to manually copy the email into another separate column to be able to then set up an integration to automate an email.

Would be good to hear back from the administrators as to when this will happen.

posts.author: lauralev (Laura Levy)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @CFcut @arganville and @jdavies-IDD! I wanted to understand a bit more the pain point here - the email column is supported by forms as well as the email integration to send out notifications to those who filled out your form. I’d love to understand more so I can pass this onward to our team to improve! Cheers!

posts.author: jdavies-IDD (John Davies)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Laura,

The process for us is to capture the email address at time of the form entry. Later in the board/process use that email address to send out a notification email with an automation. That automation maybe be two days later, it may be two weeks later. Make sense?

posts.author: lauralev (Laura Levy)
posts.cookedHtml: @jdavies-IDD - yes! The email column is not supported by automations - you would have to use the email integration to notify those who filled out the form with their email address regardless if they are monday.com users/guests/non-users. Does that help clarify? I’d like to understand the need for the email column within automation vs email integration and how that would help your use case

posts.author: jdavies-IDD (John Davies)
posts.cookedHtml: I guess the difference is the automation vs the integration. The email may be a third party, so the users/guests/non-users is good. However, we want this to be an automated process, so that we don’t have to click on the email address, fill out an email, and hit send. We know exactly what we want to “say” and who to say it too. Logic to me would say that should be an automated response. Does that help?

posts.author: lauralev (Laura Levy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jdavies-IDD -how could you automate the filling in of an email address?

have to click on the email address, fill out an email, and hit send.

Generally if the email column is supported via forms, whoever fills out the form would fill this in. You would then need to trigger the recipe to send the email.

Apologies for the back and forth but want to understand what we are missing so we can improve this

posts.author: jdavies-IDD (John Davies)
posts.cookedHtml:
No worries. “Tech support” via email can be challenging.

The email address is entered by a person filling out the form. (screen capture of form attached.) Once the form is filled out a pulse is created. The pulse is set in a status of “Inbound”. After the items are received, the status is changed
to “Received”. The desire is to have an automated email is to be sent to the person responsible for the inspection of the items. (the email address captured at the form entry) This is where there seems to be a disconnect. We have captured the email address,
but don’t see a way to have Monday send an automated email message to that address. We are not seeing that data as an option in the automation. Does that help clarify?

Screen Shot 2019-12-10 at 10.46.00 AM.png1376×2386 213 KB

posts.author: lauralev (Laura Levy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes! Super helpful - again email integration can achieve this (Outlook or Gmail - they have the same recipe):

You’ll need to choose “email column” in the someone section.

That will then send an email to the email listed in the email column upon a status change as the trigger.

Thoughts?

posts.author: jdavies-IDD (John Davies)
posts.cookedHtml: OK, so I’m not familiar with the integrations. Do we need to set up each user with the Outlook integration then? As I just clicked to add the integration, Monday/Outlook is looking for me to add my Outlook account data. So that tells me that we would have to add that integration to each individual that might be using the board, right?

posts.author: agranville (Andre Granville)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Laura.

I have a board for managing airplane tickets booked to the employees of my company. Whenever the status of the ticket changes to booked, the employee, associated to the ticket, should receive an email with the information regarding the booking.

This board is configured with a Link to item column that points to my employees board. Additionally I have set up a mirror column from where I get the employee’s email address. I would like to set up an automation that uses this email column (mirror). Unfortunately the Gmail integration recipe does not recognize the mirrored column as an option for the destination email (someone)

posts.author: lauralev (Laura Levy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @agranville - correct mirror columns are not yet supported on automations or integrations. You’d need to have the email column on your specific board for the workflow to be completed successfully. Our team is aware of the limitation with the mirror column and looking into ways to help include this as well in to automations and integrations - stay tuned!

@jdavies-IDD - the email will be sent from whoever connects to the board with their Outlook account. Only 1 person needs to set it up initially unless you want the email to come from different users within monday,com?

Cheers!

posts.author: jdavies-IDD (John Davies)
posts.cookedHtml:
OK. I see this as a work around solution. I do see one big flaw in it. If I set up my outlook account as the activated outlook account, every notification will come from me. Which then makes it difficult for someone to search their emails coming from me. It will muddy up things a bit.

I realize that the integration is a solution, but I don’t think it is a very elegant one. I also realize that this is the best we have for now. Would it be possible to request this as an added feature in the future. If we are capturing the data already it would be nice to have that field built into an automation as well as an integration. Monday is already sending emails/notifications with other automations and data/addresses gathered in the system.

posts.author: lauralev (Laura Levy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jdavies-IDD - I hear what you’re saying. Automations are entirely internal for those who are part of your monday.com account and not for sending emails externally.

Who is filling out your form? Are these people part of your monday.com account as a guest or user? Are they colleagues at your organization or outside clients?

Cheers!

posts.author: agranville (Andre Granville)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for the information Laura!

posts.author: jdavies-IDD (John Davies)
posts.cookedHtml: All should be colleagues, however not all are using Monday.

posts.author: anon34162818 (anon34162818)
posts.cookedHtml:
We use 12 integrations with gmail, 6 per email address…2 email address are collected using form.

As status of an order changes customers are sent emails with details about their order.

Super useful…should be whats needed for this request!

posts.author: ruskyAU (John)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @lauralev - I just wanted to add my voice to the current email limitations in Monday.com. This is really the primary reason stopping us from using Monday.com for more advanced app solutions. We also don’t want emails going to external (non Monday.com users/guests) from a particular Outlook/Gmail user account. We’ve even tried using the Zapier integration, however have found that the current API being used in Zapier doesn’t even allow us to achieve this requirement (raised with support and confirmed by them as a current limitation). Sending out emails in general is an area that needs much more improvement in Monday.com. Not being able to even format email bodies with HTML, tables, etc., is also a massive limitation. Looking forward to improvements in this area so that we can use Monday.com for our full business needs.

posts.author: ErickE-monday.com (Erick E.)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @ruskyAU

Thank you for sharing this with us. I understand how some of the limitation you and other users are facing may be preventing them from scaling the platform within your organization. I will share this with our integrations team so they can consider future improvements in this realm.

posts.author: andyoungy (Andy Young)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, I am just picking this up now as I am also having the problems mentioned previously. We want to update people that have completed the form when status changes but the problem with adding an external email account like gmail or outlook is the email will be coming from that email address and in our case it might get messy with people replying to an unmanned email account (as cannot integrate our exchange emails as yet).

Just keeping it on the radar in case this is still being looked at as it would certainly help us to send Monday.com emails to people not subscribed to the board. Thanks

posts.author: bysam (SB)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m voicing my vote for integrating emails from mirrored columns into integrations as well.

This is massively limiting, especially while Monday.com is pushing so hard to make Monday.com a CRM.

I can use this as a CRM, but if I take advantage of the customer database via linked boards, I am massively limited in my ability to do much of anything beyond the basic features within Monday. IE, no integrations

posts.author: mildorviolon (Jean-Francois McDonald)
posts.cookedHtml: I think Monday doesn’t want that because it would mean we no longer use the People column which is linked to a paying user. But honnestly, Monday devs, multiple users can already connect to monday with the same email, so people already have a way to go around that the One-email one-person login. So forcing a complete monthly payment for a simple notification email is a bit much in my book. You would have much more people using the notifications if you would let us notify to custom email addresses. Workflows create usage and usage can be monetised, not the number of users. There are other ways to make us pay for features / amount of actions, I think the « seats » system is a failed one because automation and notifications are at the core of what people expect from Monday right out of the box. That’s my personal opinion.

posts.author: KathleenReynolds (Kathleen Reynolds)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, I too am voicing support (and need) for automated email via forms functionality. We need to be able to do the following:

  • Send an automated confirmation email of form data submitted (1st contact by any external user).
    For us, this would be via website. (i.e submitting support tickets, contact us, research requests, sales or press inquiries, etc.)

  • Send an automated response email when the status of their request/inquiry/support ticket changes (i.e. your ticket has been assigned, here is a link to the demo you requested, your research is now complete! Click here for the results, etc.)

  • Send different automated responses based upon logic of selection in the form. (i.e. user wants to demo products ABC, and XYZ, out of form selection options: ABC, DEF, GHI, JKL, MNO, and XYZ. User received pre-scripted email response with links to those demo products, and ONLY those products, as opposed to all products. vs. Current Inefficient Workflow: Website using 3rd party form that provides logic based automated email responses. Form data is emailed to website Admin, who forwards email to Sales Team, who manually enters into Monday Sales Lead board for further follow-up. Task assigned to operator, follows-up as workload allows, composes external email to client. Goes back to Monday and updates task item often forgetting to enter/add unique details to further future sales goals. )

posts.author: JustinBerry (Justin Berry)
posts.cookedHtml: Is this still not possible?

posts.author: jadenb (Jaden Baird)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, I too am in need of this functionality. I am trying to use Monday as a tracking/informing system for COVID and I need to send letters to people who are not a part of my direct organization (think Unions presidents) and who are not nor will not be connected to Monday ever.

I need to be able to automate an email to the Union Reps when someone submits a form and selects the appropriate union representative.

posts.author: Marvinh85 (Marvin Houweling)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @lauralev, Can you tell me if there is any progress on this? I’m also looking for a solution to automate sending e-mails based on a e-mail column.

Thanks!

Marvin

posts.author: mviquez (Michael Viquez)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s hard to believe that this is something that people have been asking for for 3 years now. This does not seem like a niche application. I will ask, even though it’s probably a no, has there been any progress on this?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! We apologise for the delay in updating this thread and recognise it has been a significant amount of time since last updated - we are improving our response processes in the forum so appreciate your patience with us I just wanted to jump in and mention that from my understanding of this feature request, it is now supported in the platform - the ability to send automated emails to the email column:

Screen Shot 2023-03-29 at 1.31.30 pm1042×606 26.9 KB

Let me know if I have misunderstood!

posts.author: Yash.Gangani (Yash Gangani)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @jdavies-IDD

Check out EasyMails where you can use board columns as a dynamic data for mail.
You can design your email templates using drag and drop editor from this app.
